Faults.message:
We're sorry about the power cut in the area, we can see this is not the first time this has happened and we apologise for the repeat issues. The power cut is affecting some properties in the area shown on the map, but not all. This is why you may notice some of your neighbours have power, but you don't. It is normal to see 1 in 3 homes affected. We are working with our engineers to fix the problem as quickly as possible, and well keep you updated here on PowerTrack.

We've received an update from our engineers, they have advised that routine fixes haven't worked as planned, and we need to go in for full repairs. We have resourced an underground specialist, who will be on site by 16:30. At this stage, we are expecting your power to return by 00:30 17/04/2026.

The power cut has been caused by a fault on our underground network. Issues like this do happen, and our engineers are very experienced at fixing them. We'll keep you updated along the way.

If you need more information, please send us a message on Whatsapp or call us on 105 and quote reference 'SZ7970'

We have had reports of damage to our network by a third-party which has in turn caused the power to go off in your area. We know where this damage has taken place and our teams are working hard to make the situation safe, repair the damage and get your power back on. These type of faults often require extensive work and specialist teams which is why the restoration time seems so far away. If we can restore power sooner, we promise we will do so.

If you need more information, please send us a message on Whatsapp or call us on 105 and quote reference 'SZ8034'

This is why you may notice some of your neighbours have power, but you don't. It is normal to see 1 in 3 homes affected. We are working with our engineers to fix the problem as quickly as possible, and well keep you updated here on PowerTrack.

Engineers have arrived in the area. They are working hard to fix the problem and will get your power back on as quickly as possible. At this stage, we're aiming to have your power back on by 19:00 16/04/2026. If this changes, we'll let you know.

The cause of this power cut is a fault on our underground network. This is the most common type of fault we see and our teams are very experienced in dealing with these issues. In most cases, our engineers are able to restore power by simply replacing a fuse at the local substation, however, if the fault is severe, this may result in us needing to make full repairs which can take longer. If this does happen, we will let you know.

If you need more information, please send us a message on Whatsapp or call us on 105 and quote reference 'SZ5987'

We have had reports of damage to our network. When this happens, the power will automatically turn off to keep everyone safe.
